why_now: Codeberg adoption is flat in the current measurement window.
Owned content still contained stale install/run instructions that could break evaluator conversion.
The active repair contract explicitly requires all public-facing content to keep Codeberg primary and avoid mirror-first confusion.
repairs: Replaced deprecated npm/brew and `ralph run --spec` guidance with the current `pipx install ralph-workflow`, `ralph --init`, `ralph --diagnose`, and `ralph` flow.
Strengthened Codeberg-first CTA language in owned content endings.
Added a regression test that fails if public conversion surfaces reintroduce deprecated commands or mention the GitHub mirror without Codeberg.
verification: grep -RIn "npm install -g ralphworkflow\|brew install ralphworkflow\|ralph run --spec" content/posts drafts/2026-05-20_ai-engineering-pipeline_telegraph.md README.md START_HERE.md docs || true
expected_outcome: Reduce evaluator drop-off from stale setup instructions and keep Codeberg as the default repo destination for owned-content traffic.
replacement_condition: If Codeberg adoption is still flat after the next review window, stop treating owned-content command hygiene as the next best move and switch back to a fresh external demand-capture lane with a live execution path.
